executive_summary.one_paragraph: EGFR is a single-pass type I transmembrane receptor with exceptionally well-documented plasma membrane accessibility. Live-cell flow cytometry across >44 cancer cell lines, surface biotinylation with mass spectrometry, non-permeabilized immunofluorescence, and IHC with explicit membranous scoring criteria—combined with cryo-EM/X-ray structures of the ECD and multiple approved/clinical therapeutics engaging that domain—establish high-confidence surface accessibility. Expression is broad across solid tumor types and multiple normal epithelia. Key caveats are ligand-induced internalization that transiently reduces surface density, a predicted secreted isoform, and junctional/ciliary subdomain enrichment in polarized epithelia.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: EGFR surface accessibility is supported by multiple independent direct method types across numerous sources. (1) Live-cell flow cytometry (non-permeabilized) on glioblastoma (GSC83, U373VIII, U-87 MG), pancreatic cancer (BxPC-3, multiple lines), breast cancer (MDA-MB-231), lung cancer (NCI-H1930), and 44 cancer cell lines confirms plasma membrane surface expression (a1_evi_13–21, 47). (2) Non-permeabilized immunofluorescence with explicit permeabilization controls confirms surface vs. intracellular EGFR (a1_evi_23). (3) Surface biotinylation (sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin or streptavidin pulldown + WB/MS) in DCDMLs, prostate cancer, NIKS keratinocytes, LNCaP/PC3-PSMA, and iPSC-derived neurons (a1_evi_29–36, 45). (4) Mass spectrometry surfaceome (biotinylation+LC-MS/MS) in iPSC-derived sensory neurons (a1_evi_36). (5) IHC with explicit membranous staining criteria in tumor tissues (a1_evi_24–25, 27–28). Topology is structurally confirmed by cryo-EM, X-ray crystallography, and engineered ECD/ICD tag constructs. No meaningful contradictions; intracellular trafficking noted but does not negate robust plasma membrane pool.

U373VIII glioma cells — high EGFR/EGFRvIII surface expression detected uniformly
U373 glioma cells — low EGFR/EGFRvIII surface expression

Multiple pancreatic cancer cell lines — heterogeneous but positive EGFR surface expression by flow cytometry histograms

MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells — EGFR surface levels measured by flow with orthogonal detection antibody after HFn6-ZEGFR treatment

Prostate cancer cells — EGFR detected by sBioSITe surface biotinylation + LC-MS with 19 biotinylation sites; known marker of prostate cancer bone dissemination

SiHa (HPV16+) and HeLa (HPV18+) cervical cancer cells after E6/E7 knockdown — plasma membrane protein biotinylation + WB methodology applied

Lung squamous carcinoma tumor tissue vs. normal lung and skin; IHC staining for EGFR co-expression with B7-H3 (membranous pattern implied but permeabilization status not specified)
Normal human skin tissue; IHC showing EGFR expression in keratinocytes (contrast to B7-H3 absence in normal skin)
Sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) tumor specimens; IHC with membranous staining criterion for EGFR (31/32 cases, 97%) and p-EGFR (22/32 cases, 69%)
Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) tissue; IHC with scored membranous staining rubric (scores 1–3) defining positive EGFR surface expression

therapeutic_engagement.description: Multiple approved and clinical-stage therapeutics engage EGFR at the cell surface. Cetuximab (FDA-approved for colorectal and head/neck cancers) binds the EGFR ECD directly, confirmed by crystal structure (PDB: 1YY9) and used as an EY-photocatalyst vehicle for surface labeling on live A549 cells. Matuzumab (Phase II) ECD complex is resolved (PDB: 3C09). A bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and NKG2D was tested on BxPC-3 pancreatic cancer cells by live-cell flow cytometry. Anti-EGFR nanobody EgB4 engages the full ectodomain (PDB: 7OM4). Small-molecule TKI gefitinib is also documented in surfaceome profiling context.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: EGFR is a single-pass type I transmembrane receptor; all documented therapeutics (cetuximab, matuzumab, bispecifics, nanobodies) engage its large extracellular domain (ECD, aa25–645) displayed at the plasma membrane. A predicted secreted isoform (HPA annotation) exists, but all therapeutic programs in this ledger target the membrane-anchored form. Crystal and cryo-EM structures confirm the ECD is the drug-binding surface.

contradicting_evidence.claim: Flow cytometry surface staining on live, non-permeabilized HCT 116 colorectal cancer cells shows minimal EGFR surface expression, corroborated by near-zero RNA expression in CCLE (log2[TPM+1]=0.0). This indicates that EGFR is essentially absent from the surface of this specific cell line.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: HCT 116 is a well-documented EGFR-low/negative cell line and was deliberately used as a negative control to validate assay specificity. The absence of surface EGFR in HCT 116 reflects a cell-line-specific transcriptional silencing rather than any general property of the EGFR protein, which is robustly surface-expressed in many other cell types and tumors.

anatomical_accessibility.context: polarized epithelium (MDCK kidney epithelial cell model)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: In polarized MDCK epithelial cells, EGFR distributes ~40:60 apical:basolateral, meaning meaningful apical (luminal) fraction exists alongside basolateral. Neither pole is exclusively dominant; systemic binders reach basolateral face but apical fraction is partially restricted by tight junctions.

anatomical_accessibility.context: polarized epithelium (MDCK kidney epithelial cell model)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: EGFR shows slight basolateral enrichment (~60%) in polarized MDCK epithelium. The basolateral membrane is exposed to the blood/interstitial compartment, making systemically delivered binders accessible to this fraction of EGFR.

HPA flags EGFR as a junctional protein with enrichment at cell junctions. Junction-restricted surface domains are physically shielded by tight junction complexes, limiting access of systemically delivered large binders such as antibodies.

HPA annotation places EGFR at the primary cilium, a specialized apical surface compartment with a diffusion barrier at its base. Systemically delivered binders have restricted access to ciliary membrane domains.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal lung tissue and normal skin (non-malignant epithelium)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Lung squamous cell carcinoma tumor tissue
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR and B7-H3 are co-expressed at the surface of lung squamous carcinoma cells; EGFR is present in normal skin but B7-H3 is absent there, and neither is co-expressed in normal lung.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR surface co-expression with B7-H3 is tumor-specific in lung squamous carcinoma, making dual-targeting strategies accessible specifically in the tumor context while EGFR alone remains accessible in normal epithelium.

accessibility_modulation.change: 97% of SIP cases show positive plasma membrane EGFR staining; 69% show phosphorylated (activated) EGFR at the surface, indicating both upregulated total and activated EGFR surface presence in this tumor type.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Very high EGFR surface accessibility in SIP tumors; activated p-EGFR at the surface in the majority of cases further confirms accessible, signaling-competent receptor for therapeutic targeting.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Wild-type NIKS normal human keratinocytes (basal EGFR surface levels)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: NIKS keratinocytes expressing HPV16 E7 oncoprotein
accessibility_modulation.change: HPV16 E7 expression modestly upregulates surface EGFR via an AP2-tyrosine binding motif-dependent mechanism that alters endocytic recycling, increasing total surface EGFR levels as measured by surface biotinylation.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: HPV-driven viral oncogene expression increases EGFR surface density in keratinocytes, enhancing binder accessibility at the plasma membrane in HPV-infected/transformed epithelial cells.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Cancer cells with baseline surface EGFR and active ZNRF3/RNF43 E3 ubiquitin ligases
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: ZNRF3 overexpression (reduces surface EGFR) or ZNRF3/RNF43 knockout (increases surface EGFR and tumorigenesis)
accessibility_modulation.change: ZNRF3/RNF43-mediated ubiquitination regulates EGFR surface levels: overexpression reduces surface EGFR, while knockout increases surface EGFR signaling, demonstrating E3 ligase-dependent post-translational control of surface abundance.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR surface availability is dynamically controlled by ubiquitin-mediated trafficking; loss of ZNRF3/RNF43 in tumors elevates surface EGFR density, increasing accessibility for therapeutic binders.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Untreated EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma cells (HCC827, PC9, H1650) with baseline surfaceome
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: EGFR TKI treatment (gefitinib or osimertinib) in EGFR-mutant LUAD cells
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR TKI treatment remodels the surfaceome of EGFR-mutant LUAD cells, altering the spectrum of surface-accessible proteins including changes relevant to combination therapy opportunities.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: TKI-induced surfaceome remodeling may expose or obscure epitopes on EGFR and co-expressed surface targets, with implications for selecting combination therapy partners based on post-TKI surface accessibility.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Resting cells with ligand-free surface EGFR
accessibility_modulation.change: EGF binding triggers EGFR internalization and lysosomal degradation, reducing surface EGFR levels. Inhibition of lysosomal function (e.g., chloroquine) rescues surface EGFR by blocking degradation.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Ligand-induced internalization transiently reduces surface EGFR accessibility; in high-EGF microenvironments (e.g., tumors), surface EGFR may be dynamically depleted, affecting binder engagement.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Non-polarized or unpolarized epithelial cells with uniform surface EGFR
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Polarized MDCK epithelial cells forming apical-basolateral membrane domains
accessibility_modulation.change: In polarized MDCK epithelial cells, EGFR distributes with an approximately 40:60 apicobasal ratio, showing slight basolateral enrichment but substantial presence on both membrane faces — less strongly polarized than many RTK interaction partners.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR is accessible from both apical and basolateral surfaces in polarized epithelium, but slight basolateral enrichment suggests basolateral access is marginally favored; binders delivered luminally (apically) still encounter meaningful receptor.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Broadly expressed cells under normal conditions
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Same cells — steady-state distribution includes both plasma membrane and Golgi apparatus pools; secreted isoform also predicted
accessibility_modulation.change: HPA annotation places EGFR at plasma membrane, cell junctions, primary cilium, basal body, and Golgi apparatus, with a predicted secreted form, indicating a split between surface and intracellular/secreted pools.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: The intracellular Golgi pool and secreted isoform are not accessible to surface-targeted binders; only the plasma membrane, junctional, and ciliary pools are targetable from outside the cell, potentially reducing effective surface density.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Healthy control iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons (normal peripheral nervous system)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Familial dysautonomia (FD) patient iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR is among the most significantly deregulated proteins (p<0.01) in the plasma membrane proteome of FD sensory-like neurons compared to healthy controls, with transcriptional differences confirmed by qPCR.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Altered EGFR surface levels in FD neurons suggest disease-state-dependent changes in neuronal EGFR accessibility; this may be relevant for neuro-targeted therapeutics in hereditary sensory neuropathy contexts.

surface_bind.attribution: © Marchand, Khakzad, Correia et al. — EPFL / Inria / Novo Nordisk

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: EGFR reaches the plasma membrane autonomously as a single-pass RTK. ZNRF3/RNF43 ubiquitin ligases modulate surface density but are not required for surface delivery. No obligate co-receptor for trafficking is documented in the ledger.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: HPA annotates EGFR at cell junctions and primary cilium in addition to the general plasma membrane. In polarized MDCK epithelial cells EGFR distributes ~40:60 apical:basolateral—accessible from both faces but junctional enrichment may partially shield epitopes from large binders in intact polarized epithelium.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: The canonical EGFR ECD spans residues 25–645 (~620 aa), structurally resolved by X-ray and cryo-EM (PDB: 3NJP, 7OM4, 7SYD). Multiple approved antibodies (cetuximab, PDB 1YY9) and nanobodies engage distinct epitopes on this large extracellular surface.

epitope_masking.rationale: EGFR ECD adopts ligand-dependent conformational changes (tethered vs. extended) that expose or occlude the dimerization arm. EGF binding triggers internalization, transiently depleting surface-accessible receptor. Junctional enrichment may additionally shield epitopes from systemic binders in polarized epithelium.

evidence.claim: HPA database annotation confirms EGFR subcellular localization at plasma membrane, Golgi apparatus, cell junctions, basal body, primary cilium, and predicted secreted form (isoform 2); multiple compartments noted including endosomal/intracellular trafficking locations. Predicted secreted form noted as potential shed/secreted isoform.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Golgi apparatus;Plasma membrane;Basal body;Cell Junctions;End piece;Mid piece;Primary cilium;Principal piece;Predicted to be secreted.

evidence.claim: HPA curated flag confirms EGFR is plasma-membrane accessible; direct HPA curatorial call supporting surface accessibility.

spans.quote: HPA flags this gene as plasma-membrane accessible.

evidence.claim: HPA plasma-membrane reliability tier is 'supported' for EGFR, indicating curated confidence in the surface detection evidence base.

spans.quote: HPA plasma-membrane reliability tiers: supported.

evidence.claim: EGFR topology confirmed by engineered construct design: N-terminal Flag tag placed on the ECD (extracellular face) and HaloTag placed on the C-terminal ICD (intracellular face), establishing that the N-terminus of the mature receptor is extracellular and the C-terminus is intracellular — single-pass type I topology (signal peptide aa1-24, ECD aa25-645, TM aa646-668, ICD aa669-1210). Cell line: A549 (human lung adenocarcinoma).

spans.quote: To label EGFR on either side of the membrane with the EY photocatalyst (Fig. 1a ), we fused a Flag tag on the N terminus of the ECD of EGFR and a HaloTag on the C terminus of the ICD, designated Flag–EGFR and EGFR–HaloTag (Flag–EGFR–HaloTag), respectively (Supplementary Fig. 1a ).

evidence.claim: Full-length EGFR (residues 1–1210) and its extracellular domain (residues 25–645) were separately cloned for expression studies, confirming canonical single-pass I topology with ECD spanning aa25–645, consistent with signal peptide cleavage at aa24.

spans.quote: The full-length EGFR (residues 1–1210) and its extracellular domain (residues 25–645) were cloned into a CMV-FLAG expression vector.

evidence.claim: EGFR ectodomain crystal structure (PDB: 3NJP) used as starting point for molecular dynamics simulations; confirms experimentally resolved extracellular domain and its untethered conformation. Single-pass topology with extracellular N-terminal domain accessible to solvent.

spans.quote: For the starting structures of our EGFR simulations (simulations 1, 2, 5, and 6), four frames were selected randomly from an equilibration simulation of an untethered EGFR ectodomain crystal structure (PDB ID: 3NJP); the double mutation C271A/C283A was introduced for simulations 5 and 6.

evidence.claim: Cryo-EM reconstruction of EGFR receptor complex shows strongest density in the ectodomain region, confirming the extracellular domain is structurally ordered and accessible, with weaker density in TM and intracellular domains — consistent with single-pass topology where ECD projects extracellularly.

spans.quote: As reported in all previously published cryo-EM reconstructions of other RTKs ( Diwanji et al., 2021 ; Huang et al., 2021 ; Nielsen et al., 2022 ; Li et al., 2019 ; Uchikawa et al., 2019 ; Krimmer et al., 2023 ), the cryo-EM density was the strongest in the ectodomain region of the receptor complex and the weakest within the transmembrane and intracellular domains ( Figure 1b–c , Figure 1—figure supplement 2a, f , Figure 1—figure supplement 3a, f ).

evidence.claim: Cryo-EM structures of EGFR ectodomains adopt a characteristic 'heart-shaped' arrangement in liganded HER receptor dimers, confirming the large extracellular domain presents an accessible dimerization arm at the cell surface. Consistent with multiple prior X-ray and cryo-EM structures.

spans.quote: In both structures, the ectodomains adopt a characteristic ‘heart-shaped’ arrangement observed in previously solved X-ray and cryo-EM structures of liganded HER receptor dimers ( Garrett et al., 2002 ; Liu et al., 2012 ; Ogiso et al., 2002 ; Diwanji et al., 2021 ; Bai et al., 2023 ; Freed et al., 2017 ; Huang et al., 2021 ; Lu et al., 2010 ).

evidence.claim: Crystal structure of EGFR extracellular domain (PDB: 4UV7) superimposed on EGFR-Matuzumab complex (PDB: 3C09) confirms the ECD structure is resolved and accessible for antibody engagement at the extracellular face.

spans.quote: The crystal structure of the extracellular domain of EGFR from PDB:4UV7 was superimposed on to the EGFR-Matuzumab complex (PDB: 3C09) to facilitate these calculations.

evidence.claim: Crystal structure of full EGFR ectodomain in ternary complex with EgB4 nanobody and EGF resolved to 6.0 Å (PDB: 7OM4), confirming the complete extracellular domain architecture and its accessibility for nanobody/antibody binding at the cell surface.

spans.quote:
S 2
To study the mechanism by which the EgB4 nanobody interacts with EGFR, we then determined the structure of the full ectodomain EGFR-EgB4-EGF ternary complex from a crystal that diffracted to a maximum resolution of 6.0 Å (PDB: 7OM4; Table 1 and Fig.

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement: X-ray crystal structures of Cetuximab Fab (PDB: 1YY9) and Matuzumab Fab (PDB: 3C09) bound to EGFR extracellular domain confirm direct ECD engagement by two approved/clinical-stage anti-EGFR therapeutic antibodies. Both antibodies engage the extracellular face of membrane-bound EGFR. Cetuximab is FDA-approved for colorectal and head/neck cancers; Matuzumab reached Phase II clinical trials.

spans.quote: The full-length IgM models for Cetuximab and Matuzumab were built using structural data available in the PDB: i) the X-ray crystal structures of Cetuximab and Matuzumab Fab bound to the human EGFR extracellular domains (ECDs) (PDB: 1YY9 ( 56 ) and 3C09 ( 22 ), respectively), ii) the cryogenic electron microscopy (cryo-EM) structure of the human IgM Fc pentamer (PDB: 6KXS) ( 25 ), and iii) the crystal structure of mouse Cµ2 domain (PDB: 4JVU) ( 57 ).

evidence.claim: Complete EGFR ECD structure obtained from cryo-EM (PDB: 7SYD) used for modeling IgM and IgG antibody complexes with EGFR, confirming ECD is structurally resolved and serves as direct target for therapeutic antibody engagement at the cell surface.

spans.quote: To model the bound systems of IgM and IgG antibodies in complex with the EGFR, we obtained the complete EGFR ECD structure from a cryo-EM structure (PDB:7SYD) ( 62 ).

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry on live glioma cells (non-permeabilized) detects uniform EGFR antigen on the surface of GSC83 and U373VIII cells (high EGFR/EGFRvIII expression) and U373 (low expression). Paired confocal IF shows intact vs. permeabilized staining comparison. Method: live-cell flow cytometry + non-permeabilized vs. permeabilized IF. Cell lines: GSC83, U373VIII, U373.

spans.quote: (A) Flow cytometry analysis illustrating the uniform presence of EGFR antigen on the surface of glioma cells with high (GSC83 and U373VIII) and low (U373) levels of EGFR/EGFRvIII expression (right panel—dot plot; left panel—histogram); (B) Confocal imaging of immunofluorescent staining for EGFR antigen (red) and CD9 (green) in adherent GSC83 cells either intact or permeabilized before antibody exposure.

evidence.claim: EGFR/EGFRvIII is detectable principally on the cell surface of isolated GSC83 glioblastoma cells, as confirmed by flow cytometry (live cells, non-permeabilized) and confocal imaging. Surface decoration is described as uniform/even. Supports robust plasma membrane localization in glioblastoma.

spans.quote: In isolated GSC83 cells the endogenously expressed EGFR/EGFRvIII (Spinelli et al. 2024 ) appears to be detectable principally on the cell surface, which is evenly decorated with the EGFR antigen, as revealed by flow cytometry and confocal imaging.

evidence.claim: Live-cell flow cytometry assay demonstrates EGFR cell-surface binding on BxPC-3 pancreatic cancer cells, using a therapeutic bispecific antibody construct. Method: flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells; confirms EGFR surface accessibility in pancreatic cancer cell line.

spans.quote: ( D ) Cell-surface binding assessed by flow cytometry: binding to EGFR on BxPC-3 cells and to NKG2D on primary human NK cells.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry histograms of surface EGFR across multiple pancreatic cancer cell lines confirm heterogeneous but positive EGFR surface expression in pancreatic cancer. Method: live-cell surface flow cytometry (non-permeabilized cells).

spans.quote: ( A ) Representative flow cytometry histograms of surface EGFR on the indicated pancreatic cancer cell lines.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry surface staining on HCT 116 cells shows minimal EGFR surface expression, consistent with near-zero RNA expression (CCLE log2[TPM+1]=0.0); used as negative control. Validates specificity of surface flow cytometry assay for EGFR.

spans.quote: HCT 116 served as a negative control and showed minimal surface staining, consistent with a CCLE value of log2[TPM+1] = 0.0.

evidence.claim: Live-cell flow cytometry confirms surface EGFR expression in NCI-H1930 cells despite granular/diffuse IHC pattern in clinical specimens, demonstrating that flow cytometry on intact cells is a more reliable surface-accessibility readout than IHC membranous staining pattern alone.

spans.quote: In addition, while clinical specimens often exhibited a granular and/or diffuse IHC pattern, rather than a sharp membranous staining, NCI–H1930 cells showed a similar pattern yet demonstrated cell surface expression by flow cytometry.

evidence.claim: EGFR surface levels quantified by live-cell flow cytometry on U-87 MG glioblastoma cells (high EGFR/TfR1 expression) after 24h incubation with HFn6-Z EGFR construct. Confirms EGFR is measurably present on the plasma membrane surface of glioblastoma cells.

spans.quote: U-87 MG cells, a glioblastoma cell line with high expression of both EGFR and TfR1, were incubated separately with different HFn6-Z EGFR samples for 24 h, after which the surface levels of EGFR were quantified via flow cytometry.

evidence.claim: EGFR surface levels measured by live-cell flow cytometry using an orthogonal detection antibody in U-87 MG (glioblastoma) and MDA-MB-231 (breast cancer) cells; cross-cell-line validation of surface EGFR detection with antibody specificity noted (orthogonal antibody used for detection).

spans.quote: U-87 MG and MDA-MB-231 cells were treated with HFn6-Z EGFR for 24 h, and the surface levels of EGFR were measured via flow cytometry with an orthogonal detection antibody.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry analysis of EGFR protein expression on the surface of 44 cancer cell lines (various solid tumor types) using intact cells; large-scale surface expression survey confirming EGFR plasma membrane accessibility across multiple cancer lineages.

spans.quote: Fig. 1 Co-expression of B7-H3 and EGFR in solid tumors. a , b Analysis of B7-H3 and EGFR RNA expression across ( a ) different cancer types and ( b ) all cancers using the TCGA dataset. c Flow cytometry analysis of B7-H3 and EGFR protein expressions in various cancer cell lines ( n = 44 cell lines).

evidence.claim: Immunofluorescent staining (non-permeabilized conditions implied by co-localization on cell surface) demonstrates co-localization of EGFR with B7-H3 on the same cancer cells, confirming surface-accessible EGFR in solid tumor cells.

spans.quote: Immunofluorescent staining demonstrated co-localization of EGFR with B7-H3 on the same cancer cells (Fig. 1f ).

evidence.claim: Immunofluorescence microscopy comparing non-permeabilized (surface EGFR) and permeabilized (intracellular EGFR) conditions side-by-side; confirms that EGFR is detectable on the cell surface of intact cells, with quantified data from n=2 independent experiments (mean±SD, p<0.01 by multiple t-tests). Method family: IF with explicit permeabilization control.

spans.quote: Representative immunofluorescence micrographs (A) show either cell surface EGFR in nonpermeabilized cells or intracellular EGFR in permeabilized cells as indicated and (B) quantified data from n = 2 independent experiments (mean ± SD; ∗∗, p < 0.01 using multiple t tests; Prism).

evidence.claim: IHC with scoring for membranous EGFR staining (positive = moderate/strong membranous staining in >10% tumor cells) shows positive plasma membrane staining in 31/32 sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) cases (97%), confirming high-frequency EGFR surface expression in this tumor type.

spans.quote: EGFR showed positive plasma membrane staining in 31 of 32 SIP cases (97%).

evidence.claim: Phosphorylated EGFR (p-EGFR) detected by IHC with positive plasma membrane staining in 22/32 SIP cases (69%), confirming that active (phosphorylated) EGFR is present and accessible at the plasma membrane in sinonasal inverted papilloma.

spans.quote: For p-EGFR, 22 of 32 SIP cases (69%) showed positive plasma membrane staining.

evidence.claim: IHC for EGFR and p-EGFR scored as positive when moderate-to-strong membranous staining is observed in >10% of tumor cells; membranous staining criterion explicitly applied, establishing that this assay detects surface-localized EGFR protein. Based on Menendez M et al. scoring rubric.

spans.quote: IHC for EGFR and p-EGFR was considered positive when moderate to strong membranous staining was observed in more than 10% of tumor cells; cases that did not meet this criterion were regarded as negative, based on Menendez M et al. [ 23 ].

evidence.claim: IHC staining confirms EGFR protein co-expression with B7-H3 in lung squamous carcinoma tumor tissue but not in normal lung or skin tissues; membranous staining pattern consistent with surface expression in tumor context. Method: IHC on fixed tumor sections vs normal tissue.

spans.quote: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining confirmed the co-expression of both targets in lung squamous carcinoma, but not in normal lung or skin (Fig. 1d, e ).

evidence.claim: IHC shows EGFR expression in normal skin tissues (unlike B7-H3 which is absent in normal skin), consistent with EGFR's known ubiquitous surface expression. Provides tissue selectivity context: EGFR surface expression is not tumor-restricted.

spans.quote: Notably, B7-H3 was highly expressed in lung squamous carcinoma patient samples with minimal expression in normal tissues (Fig. 1d , and Supplementary Fig. 1a–c ); whereas EGFR, but not B7-H3, was expressed in normal skin tissues (Fig. 1d , and Supplementary Fig. 1d ).

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation of DCDMLs (ductal carcinoma-derived mammary-like cells) used to collect plasma membrane proteins via streptavidin bead pulldown, followed by Western blot with 4G10 anti-phosphotyrosine antibody to assess all ErbB kinases including EGFR; demonstrates ErbB kinases accessible at the cell surface by biotinylation. Method: surface biotinylation + streptavidin pulldown + WB with phosphotyrosine detection; no permeabilization.

spans.quote: In order to simultaneously assess all possible ErbB kinases, we subjected DCDMLs to cell surface biotinylation, collected labeled plasma membrane proteins with streptavidin beads, and probed Western blots with the 4G10 antiphosphotyrosine antibody.

evidence.claim: EGFR detected in sBioSITe surface biotinylation + MS experiment with 19 biotinylation sites on prostate cancer cells; identified as a known marker of prostate cancer dissemination to bones. Surface biotinylation method (sBioSITe = BioSITe adapted for cell surface IP of biotinylated peptides). Cell type: prostate cancer (A549 used for interactome labeling per related text).

spans.quote: Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), a known marker of dissemination of prostate cancer to bones, was detected with 19 biotinylation sites [ 29 ] (Fig. 3 C).

evidence.claim: sBioSITe method (surface Biotinylation Site Identification Technology): adapts BioSITe for cell surface protein enrichment via immunoprecipitation of biotinylated peptides; enables sensitive and reliable surface protein identification. This is the methodology paper for the EGFR sBioSITe detection (19 biotinylation sites). Method family: surface_biotinylation; method_subclass: peptide-level IP + LC-MS.

spans.quote: We report the development of surface Biotinylation Site Identification Technology (sBioSITe), a sensitive and reliable method for the enrichment of cell surface proteins by adapting the Biotinylation Site Identification Technology (BioSITe) for immunoprecipitation of biotinylated peptides [ 17 ].

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation of NIKS cells (16 E7-expressing vs wild type) with streptavidin bead recovery and Western blot; EGFR and selected targets detected at higher total levels at cell surface in 16 E7-expressing NIKS cells. Method: surface biotinylation + streptavidin bead pulldown + WB. Cell line: NIKS (HPV16 E7-expressing).

spans.quote: Biotinylated proteins were recovered with streptavidin beads and analysed by Western blot; we observed higher total levels of the selected targets on the cell surface in Wt 16 E7-expressing NIKS cells ( Fig. 2 C).

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation + Western blot of plasma membrane proteins from SiHa (HPV16+) and HeLa (HPV18+) cells after E6/E7 knockdown; protocol: plasma membrane protein biotinylation followed by Western blot assessment. Confirms surface EGFR detection methodology in HPV-positive cervical cancer cell lines.

spans.quote: To investigate this, we knocked down the expression of E6 and E7 oncoproteins from HPV16 E7-expressing SiHa and HPV18 E7-expressing HeLa cells; then plasma membrane proteins were biotinylated and labelled proteins were assessed by Western blotting.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation method using sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin: cells washed with PBS then covered with sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin solution for 10 minutes at room temperature; standard cleavable biotin chemistry for surface protein capture. Used in sensory-like neuron surfaceome experiment detecting EGFR.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: sensory-like neurons (iPSC-derived, FD patients and controls)

spans.quote: In brief, cells were washed with PBS and covered with sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin solution for ten minutes at room temperature.

evidence.claim: Matured iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons incubated with sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin in vitro to label surface proteins; confirms intact cell surface biotinylation protocol applied to neuron cultures. Paired with LC-MS/MS to identify surfaceome including EGFR.

spans.quote: Sensory-like neurons were processed as follows: (A) Matured sensory-like neurons were incubated with sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin solution in vitro.

evidence.claim: Sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin surface biotinylation followed by LC-MS/MS (NanoLC on Orbitrap Fusion) identified 1,508 distinct surface proteins in iPSC-derived sensory-like neuron cultures across 3 independent experiments; EGFR detected as part of this surfaceome. High-confidence surfaceome MS result in human neurons.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons (FD patients and controls)

spans.quote: Via biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry ( Fig 1A , 1B ), we identified 1,508 distinct proteins in sensory-like neuron cultures derived from FD patients and controls, consistently observed across three independent experiments (S2 Table).

evidence.claim: Cell surface capture (CSC) method described: periodate oxidation of glycan sialic acids generates aldehydes that react with hydrazide beads to form stable hydrazone linkages, capturing glycoproteins; PNGase F release of N-glycan-modified surface proteins. This is the CSC/surfaceome MS method family used in a study detecting EGFR at the cell surface. Developers: Wollscheid, Aebersold and co-workers.

spans.quote: The aldehydes readily react with hydrazide, to form stable hydrazone linkages. 75 The enrichment technique called cell surface capture (CSC) developed by Wollscheid, Aebersold, and co-workers, utilized this chemistry to capture cell surface proteins onto hydrazide beads. 76 The captured glycoproteins were washed extensively and released using PNGase F, an amidase that cleaves between the innermost GlcNAc and the N -glycan-modified asparagine.

evidence.claim: Apicobasal surfaceome quantification using SILAC + chemoproteomics on filter-grown MDCK polarized epithelial cells; maps cell surface proteins quantitatively to apical vs basolateral compartments. Provides surfaceome MS methodology context for EGFR surface detection.

spans.quote: In order to map the apicobasal surfaceome quantitatively, we used filter-grown Madin–Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cells as the best-established in vitro system for epithelial polarity [ 16 ] in combination with chemoproteomics and stable isotope labeling of amino acids in cell culture (SILAC) ( Figure 1 A) [ 17 , 18 ].

evidence.claim: In surfaceome MS pipeline, identified surface proteins filtered for plasma membrane localization evidence using UniprotKB and Surfy surfaceome predictor; confirms EGFR designation as plasma membrane protein through multi-evidence filtering. Methodological rigor for surfaceome protein assignment.

spans.quote: Finally, identified proteins were filtered for evidence for localization at the plasma membrane using UniprotKB and the Surfy surfaceome predictor [ 19 ] ( Figure S1A ).

evidence.claim: Surfaceome profiling by MS-based cell surface proteomics applied to LUAD (lung adenocarcinoma) cell lines treated with vehicle control or gefitinib (EGFR TKI); schematic describes methodology for detecting surface protein changes downstream of EGFR inhibition. EGFR is contextually central to the experimental design.

spans.quote: Figure 1 EGFR TKI upregulates ALPP expression in LUAD cell (A) Schematic of surfaceome profiling of LUAD cell lines treated with vehicle control or gefitinib.

evidence.claim: BirA*-affibody proximity biotinylation method for EGFR: affibody ZEGFR:1907 fused to BirA* labels EGFR.Fc and neighboring proteins; biotinylated proteins monitored by streptavidin-HRP Western blot after protein G bead elution under non-reducing conditions. Method: proximity-biotinylation + WB with streptavidin-HRP (BioLegend, 1:2500). Confirms EGFR ECD accessibility for affibody targeting.

spans.quote: The level of the biotinylation of EGFR.Fc and HER2.Fc arising from exposing them to Affibody.BirA* constructs was monitored by eluting the biotinylated proteins bound to the protein G beads by boiling the beads in lithium dodecyl sulfate (LDS) sample buffer and performing Western blot on the recovered samples under nonreducing conditions using 1:2500 streptavidin-HRP.

evidence.claim: Biotinylated EGFR confirmed by Western blot using streptavidin-HRP (BioLegend, 1:2500); provides paired WB validation step for surface biotinylation assay. Antibody reagent documented.

spans.quote: The presence of biotinylated EGFR was confirmed by Western blot using 1:2500 streptavidin-HRP (BioLegend).

evidence.claim: Anti-EGFR antibody (Abcam, anti-EGFR.HRP, 1:5000) and anti-HER2 antibody (Novus Biologicals, 1:5000) used to confirm EGFR and HER2 presence in biotinylated surface pulldown fraction by Western blot. Antibody reagents documented (Abcam anti-EGFR.HRP, Novus anti-HER2.HRP). Paired WB step for surface biotinylation.

spans.quote: The presence of EGFR and HER2 among the biotinylated proteins pulled down was confirmed by Western blot using 1:5000 anti-EGFR.HRP (Abcam) and anti-HER2.HRP (Novus Biologicals) conjugates.

evidence.claim: SPR (Biacore T200) binding assay confirms affibody ZEGFR:1907.BirA* and ZHER2:243.BirA* bind their respective targets — the extracellular domains of EGFR and HER2; confirms ECD accessibility for affibody-based surface labeling approach. Method: SPR on purified ECD.

spans.quote: The ability of Z EGFR:1907 .BirA* and Z HER2:243 .BirA* to bind to their targets (the extracellular domains of EGFR and HER2, respectively) was determined by SPR (Biacore T200; GE Healthcare Mississauga, ON, Canada).

evidence.claim: Cell-surface biotinylation + streptavidin bead enrichment + Western blot used to validate GCPII/EGFR surface co-complex in LNCaP/PC3-PSMA prostate cancer cells; confirms EGFR detection by Western blot in streptavidin-enriched cell surface fraction. Method: surface biotinylation + streptavidin enrichment + WB + MS.

spans.quote: Following cell lysis and protein enrichment on streptavidin beads, we performed Western blot analysis of the resulting enriched proteins.

evidence.claim: Streptavidin-bead enrichment eluates analyzed by both Western blotting and mass spectrometry for EGFR and GCPII surface complex; confirms paired WB + MS validation of surface biotinylation experiment. Method: surface biotinylation → streptavidin enrichment → WB + MS.

spans.quote: Protein eluants after streptavidin-bead enrichment were analyzed by Western blotting and mass spectrometry.

evidence.claim: Live-cell flow cytometry on A549 cells transfected with Flag-EGFR and EGFR-HaloTag constructs detects significant anti-Flag signal on cell surface, confirming extracellular N-terminal Flag-tagged EGFR is accessible from outside the plasma membrane. Method: flow cytometry on live intact cells; anti-Flag antibody detecting ECD-displayed tag.

spans.quote: After transfecting the two constructs in A549 cells, we saw significant expression of the anti-Flag signal on cells for both constructs by flow cytometry (Supplementary Fig. 1b ).

evidence.claim: Photocatalytic EY probe attached to EGFR ECD via anti-Flag antibody targeting N-terminal Flag tag (extracellular face) on live cells; separately, HaloTag-based ICD probe used for intracellular side. Confirms asymmetric labeling from extracellular vs intracellular orientation of EGFR. Cells illuminated with blue light in presence of biotinylated photoprobes.

spans.quote: The photocatalyst, EY, is introduced to EGFR by incubating cells expressing an N-terminal Flag tag (Flag–EGFR) or a C-terminal HaloTag (EGFR–HaloTag); EY is anchored using an EY-conjugated anti-Flag antibody or a derivatized EY with an HTL, respectively.

evidence.claim: Cetuximab used as selective antibody to attach EY photocatalyst to EGFR for surface labeling (photoproximity labeling); cetuximab engages EGFR ECD on live cells at the plasma membrane. Confirms cetuximab as a surface EGFR-targeting therapeutic antibody (FDA-approved). Method: EY-cetuximab photocatalytic proximity labeling on live cells.

spans.quote: We and others have previously demonstrated labeling strategies to attach the photocatalyst using a selective antibody to the protein of interest (POI), such as cetuximab 24 , or receptor-specific ligands and protein-binding small molecules 74 – 79 .

evidence.claim: Methodological validation: ecto-tags placed on both inside and outside of EGFR reduce likelihood of blocking EGFR neighborhood interactions; confirms that N-terminal ECD tag and C-terminal ICD tag strategy does not sterically occlude epitopes. Functional integrity of surface-tagged EGFR constructs confirmed.

spans.quote: By appending genetically encoded ecto-tags on both the inside and the outside of EGFR, we were able to introduce the EY photocatalyst outside the receptor coding sequence, which reduces the likelihood of blocking potential neighborhood interactions.

evidence.claim: No detectable effects on EGFR function observed with ecto-tags (Flag on ECD, HaloTag on ICD), confirming the tagged constructs faithfully recapitulate endogenous EGFR surface presentation and function. Methodological validation that tags do not disrupt surface targeting.

spans.quote: We did not observe any detectable effects on EGFR functions with these ecto-tags, and it is prudent to verify target protein functions in cells when applying to new protein targets.

evidence.claim: In vitro biotinylation of purified EGFR ECD (aa25-645)-Avi construct using BirA enzyme confirmed by anti-biotin HRP Western blot; validates efficient biotin incorporation into EGFR extracellular domain for phage display / therapeutic antibody screening. Confirms ECD is accessible for biotin labeling.

spans.quote: In vitro biotinylation of the purified hEGFR(25–645)-Avi protein using BirA enzyme was verified by Western blotting using an HRP-conjugated anti-biotin antibody, confirming successful biotin incorporation ( Figure 2 C).

evidence.claim: Biotinylation efficiency of EGFR ECD (aa25-645)-Avi construct confirmed by Western blot using HRP-conjugated anti-biotin antibody after BirA-mediated biotinylation; paired WB step documenting methodology for surface-accessible ECD preparation.

spans.quote: After buffer exchange into storage buffer, biotinylation efficiency was confirmed by Western blotting using an HRP-conjugated anti-biotin antibody.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring rubric for EGFR membranous staining: weak membranous staining scored 1, complete but moderate membrane staining scored 2, complete and strong staining scored 3. Score of 2–3 used to define positive surface EGFR expression in NSCLC. Method: IHC on fixed tissue sections with validated membranous staining criterion.

spans.quote: Weak membranous staining was scored as 1, complete but moderate membrane staining was scored as 2, and complete and strong staining was scored as 3 ( Figure 1 and Figure 2 ).

evidence.claim: qPCR analysis of EGFR gene expression in iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from FD patients and healthy controls (4 separately prepared differentiations per group); RNA-level expression measurement without surface fractionation — non-surface evidence qualifying the surfaceome MS result (EGFR detected by surface biotinylation+MS in the same system).

spans.quote: To assess whether the observed differences in the neuronal plasma membrane were mirrored at the gene expression level, we conducted qPCR analysis for CACNA2D3, GPM6A, EGFR, and ABCA7, using samples from four separately prepared differentiations for FD and healthy control sensory-like neurons, respectively.

evidence.claim: HPA database assigns EGFR subcellular localization to plasma membrane, cell junctions, primary cilium, basal body, Golgi apparatus, and predicts secreted form. This establishes EGFR as a cell-surface protein with additional specialized surface subdomains including primary cilium and cell junctions, as well as intracellular (Golgi) and secreted isoform localization.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Golgi apparatus;Plasma membrane;Basal body;Cell Junctions;End piece;Mid piece;Primary cilium;Principal piece;Predicted to be secreted.

evidence.claim: HPA flags EGFR as a junctional protein, indicating localization restricted to or enriched at cell junctions on the plasma membrane surface. This constrains anatomical accessibility to junction-associated surface domains.

spans.quote: HPA flags this gene as junctional.

evidence.claim: EGFR shows positive plasma membrane staining by IHC in 97% (31/32) of sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) tumor cases, indicating high surface expression in this upper respiratory tract tumor type.

spans.quote: EGFR showed positive plasma membrane staining in 31 of 32 SIP cases (97%).

evidence.claim: Phosphorylated EGFR (p-EGFR) shows positive plasma membrane staining by IHC in 69% (22/32) of SIP tumor cases, indicating that activated/phosphorylated EGFR is present at the surface in the majority of sinonasal inverted papilloma tumors but at lower frequency than total EGFR.

spans.quote: For p-EGFR, 22 of 32 SIP cases (69%) showed positive plasma membrane staining.

evidence.claim: IHC of clinical samples confirms EGFR co-expression with B7-H3 in lung squamous cell carcinoma tissue but not in normal lung or normal skin, establishing tumor-specific co-expression pattern with tissue-level differential between malignant and normal tissue.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: lung squamous carcinoma tissue, normal lung, normal skin

spans.quote: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining confirmed the co-expression of both targets in lung squamous carcinoma, but not in normal lung or skin (Fig. 1d, e ).

evidence.claim: By IHC, EGFR is expressed in normal skin tissue (but B7-H3 is not), while in lung squamous carcinoma, both EGFR and B7-H3 are co-expressed with minimal B7-H3 in normal tissues. This confirms EGFR presence in normal skin and in lung squamous carcinoma, but not exclusive tumor-restriction of EGFR itself.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: normal skin, lung squamous carcinoma patient samples

spans.quote: Notably, B7-H3 was highly expressed in lung squamous carcinoma patient samples with minimal expression in normal tissues (Fig. 1d , and Supplementary Fig. 1a–c ); whereas EGFR, but not B7-H3, was expressed in normal skin tissues (Fig. 1d , and Supplementary Fig. 1d ).

evidence.claim: Immunofluorescent staining demonstrates co-localization of EGFR and B7-H3 on the same cancer cells (lung squamous carcinoma), confirming EGFR surface co-presentation with B7-H3 at the plasma membrane of tumor cells.

spans.quote: Immunofluorescent staining demonstrated co-localization of EGFR with B7-H3 on the same cancer cells (Fig. 1f ).

evidence.claim: Bioinformatics analysis (TCGA) shows statistically significant EGFR co-expression with B7-H3 across multiple tumor types including lung, head and neck, pancreatic, esophageal, thyroid, and kidney cancers, establishing EGFR expression as a feature of multiple solid tumor tissues.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: TCGA tumor cohorts: lung, head and neck, pancreatic, esophageal, thyroid, kidney cancers

cell_context.tissue: lung; head and neck; pancreas; esophagus; thyroid; kidney

spans.quote: Statistically significant co-expression was observed in lung, head and neck, pancreatic, esophageal, thyroid, and kidney cancers (Fig. 1a ).

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry demonstrates uniform EGFR antigen surface presence across glioma cell lines with high (GSC83 glioma stem cells, U373VIII) and low (U373) levels of EGFR/EGFRvIII expression. Confocal immunofluorescence of intact vs. permeabilized GSC83 cells confirms surface vs. intracellular distribution.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: GSC83, U373VIII, U373 glioma cell lines

spans.quote: (A) Flow cytometry analysis illustrating the uniform presence of EGFR antigen on the surface of glioma cells with high (GSC83 and U373VIII) and low (U373) levels of EGFR/EGFRvIII expression (right panel—dot plot; left panel—histogram); (B) Confocal imaging of immunofluorescent staining for EGFR antigen (red) and CD9 (green) in adherent GSC83 cells either intact or permeabilized before antibody exposure.

evidence.claim: In GSC83 glioma stem cells endogenously expressing EGFR/EGFRvIII, the receptor is detectable principally on the cell surface, with even surface decoration as revealed by flow cytometry and confocal imaging. This confirms predominantly plasma membrane localization in glioma stem cells.

spans.quote: In isolated GSC83 cells the endogenously expressed EGFR/EGFRvIII (Spinelli et al. 2024 ) appears to be detectable principally on the cell surface, which is evenly decorated with the EGFR antigen, as revealed by flow cytometry and confocal imaging.

evidence.claim: U-87 MG glioblastoma cells have high surface EGFR expression as quantified by flow cytometry, establishing this cell line as a high-EGFR surface-expressing glioblastoma model.

spans.quote: U-87 MG cells, a glioblastoma cell line with high expression of both EGFR and TfR1, were incubated separately with different HFn6-Z EGFR samples for 24 h, after which the surface levels of EGFR were quantified via flow cytometry.

evidence.claim: Surface EGFR levels are measurable by flow cytometry in both U-87 MG (glioblastoma) and MDA-MB-231 (triple-negative breast cancer) cell lines, confirming EGFR surface expression in these tumor cell types of brain and breast origin.

cell_context.cell_type: glioblastoma cells; triple-negative breast cancer cells

spans.quote: U-87 MG and MDA-MB-231 cells were treated with HFn6-Z EGFR for 24 h, and the surface levels of EGFR were measured via flow cytometry with an orthogonal detection antibody.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry histograms confirm surface EGFR expression across multiple pancreatic cancer cell lines, establishing EGFR as a surface-accessible antigen in pancreatic cancer cell types.

spans.quote: ( A ) Representative flow cytometry histograms of surface EGFR on the indicated pancreatic cancer cell lines.

evidence.claim: In polarized MDCK epithelial cells used as a model for epithelial polarity, EGFR is relatively evenly distributed across the apical and basolateral membranes with an apicobasal ratio of approximately 40:60, indicating slight basolateral enrichment but substantial apical surface accessibility in polarized epithelium.

spans.quote: However, we show here that both the EGF precursor and its receptor EGFR are relatively evenly distributed across the polarized membrane (with an apicobasal ratio of 50:50 and 40:60, respectively).

evidence.claim: In polarized epithelial cells (MDCK), EGFR and MET show similar apical and basolateral surface abundances (not strongly polarized), in contrast to many of their interaction partners which are strongly polarized. This indicates EGFR is accessible from both apical and basolateral faces in polarized epithelium.

spans.quote: Whereas various receptor tyrosine kinases (RTKs) that mediate major cellular functions such as growth signaling (e.g., EGFR and MET) showed rather similar apical and basolateral abundances ( Figure 2 C), many of their modulatory interaction partners were found to be strongly polarized.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation followed by Western blot analysis in NIKS (human keratinocyte) cells shows higher total surface levels of EGFR in HPV16 E7-expressing cells compared to wild-type, indicating that HPV16 E7 oncoprotein expression in keratinocytes upregulates EGFR surface abundance. Baseline cell type: normal keratinocytes; modulating state: HPV16 E7 expression; direction: surface EGFR increase.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: NIKS (normal human keratinocytes) expressing HPV16 E7

spans.quote: Biotinylated proteins were recovered with streptavidin beads and analysed by Western blot; we observed higher total levels of the selected targets on the cell surface in Wt 16 E7-expressing NIKS cells ( Fig. 2 C).

evidence.claim: In HPV16 E7-expressing keratinocytes (NIKS cells), surface EGFR is modestly upregulated via an AP2-tyrosine binding motif-dependent mechanism. Baseline: wild-type NIKS keratinocytes with basal EGFR surface levels; modulating state: HPV16 E7 expression altering endocytic recycling; direction: modest surface EGFR upregulation. This demonstrates accessibility modulation by viral oncoprotein-induced alteration of endocytic trafficking.

spans.quote: Remarkably, we observed several potential membrane proteins up- and down-regulated by the AP2-tyrosine binding motif in E7, including a modest upregulation of the surface EGFR ( Fig. 2 B), which is in agreement with our previous results [ 13 ].

evidence.claim: EGFR TKI treatment (gefitinib, osimertinib) remodels the surfaceome of EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) cells. This study establishes that drug-induced cell state changes alter surface protein accessibility in LUAD, with EGFR pathway inhibition as the modulating condition.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: EGFR-mutant LUAD cell lines (HCC827, PC9, H1650)

spans.quote: We aimed to assess the extent to which EGFR inhibitors remodel the surfaceome in LUADs and whether such remodeling provides an opportunity for combination therapy.

evidence.claim: ZNRF3 overexpression reduces EGFR surface levels in cancer cells, while ZNRF3/RNF43 knockout upregulates EGFR surface signaling and promotes tumorigenesis. This demonstrates that EGFR surface availability is regulated by ZNRF3/RNF43 E3 ubiquitin ligases: baseline in cancer cells; modulating state: ZNRF3 gain or loss of function; direction of surface change: ZNRF3 OE reduces surface EGFR, ZNRF3 KO increases surface EGFR.

spans.quote: Overexpression of ZNRF3 reduces EGFR levels and suppresses cancer cell growth in vitro and in vivo, whereas knockout of ZNRF3/RNF43 stimulates cell growth and tumorigenesis through upregulated EGFR signaling.

evidence.claim: EGF stimulation triggers EGFR internalization from the cell surface and lysosomal degradation. This ligand-induced endocytosis reduces surface EGFR availability: baseline state has surface EGFR; EGF stimulation is the modulating condition; direction is surface EGFR decrease due to internalization and degradation. Lysosomal function inhibition (chloroquine) rescues surface EGFR levels.

spans.quote: A , Schematic of epidermal growth factor (EGF)-induced epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) internalization and lysosomal degradation, with or without inhibition of lysosomal function by chloroquine.

evidence.claim: EGFR is identified as a significantly deregulated protein (p<0.01) in sensory-like neuron plasma membrane proteomics comparing familial dysautonomia (FD) patients to healthy controls, implicating EGFR expression in sensory neurons and its potential alteration in peripheral neuropathy.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons (FD patients and healthy controls)

spans.quote: Among the most deregulated proteins (p < 0.01), we identified several neuronal candidates with a potential link to nerve pathology such as: calcium voltage-gated channel auxiliary subunit alpha2delta 3 (CACNA2D3, p < 0.01) [ 52 ], neuronal membrane glycoprotein M6-A (GPM6A, p < 0.001) [ 53 , 54 ], ATP-binding cassette subfamily A member 7 (ABCA7) [ 55 ], and epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR, p < 0.01) [ 56 , 57 ].

evidence.claim: EGFR mRNA expression differences observed in the neuronal surface proteome between familial dysautonomia (FD) and healthy control iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons were validated by qPCR, confirming transcriptional-level differences in EGFR expression in this neuronal context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons (FD patients and healthy controls)

spans.quote: To assess whether the observed differences in the neuronal plasma membrane were mirrored at the gene expression level, we conducted qPCR analysis for CACNA2D3, GPM6A, EGFR, and ABCA7, using samples from four separately prepared differentiations for FD and healthy control sensory-like neurons, respectively.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry identifies 1,508 distinct surface proteins in iPSC-derived sensory-like neuron cultures from familial dysautonomia (FD) patients and healthy controls, providing the experimental context for EGFR's detection in the neuronal surface proteome.

spans.quote: Via biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry ( Fig 1A , 1B ), we identified 1,508 distinct proteins in sensory-like neuron cultures derived from FD patients and controls, consistently observed across three independent experiments (S2 Table).

evidence.claim: EGFR is in the top 84th percentile of expression in the surfaceome analysis of HPV-altered penile/glans tissue, indicating high EGFR expression in HPV-associated disease context of squamous epithelial tissue.

spans.quote: Of interest, the previously described epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) was in the top 84% of expression.

evidence.claim: MDA-MB-231 (triple-negative breast adenocarcinoma) and SK-OV-3 (ovarian cancer) cell lines are used as EGFR-expressing models, confirming EGFR expression in breast and ovarian cancer cell-line contexts.

spans.quote: The human breast adenocarcinoma cell lines MDA-MB-231 and SK-OV-3 were used as representative cell lines expressing EGFR and both HER2 and EGFR.

confidence_reasoning: A1 grades this as direct_multi_method with live-cell flow cytometry across >44 cell lines, surface biotinylation+MS in multiple cell types, non-permeabilized IF with quantified controls, membranous IHC in patient tissues, and structural confirmation by cryo-EM/X-ray. The single contradicting entry (HCT116 absence) is cell-line-specific and was used as a deliberate negative control. A2's state-dependence signals (ligand-induced internalization, ZNRF3 regulation, TKI-induced surfaceome remodeling) introduce moderate contextual variability but do not negate robust constitutive plasma membrane expression. Secreted isoform is annotation-only with no quantitative ratio. Overall confidence is high.
